🛒 Superstore Sales & Profit Analysis- Exploratory Data Analysis (EDA)¶

📌 Project Overview¶

This project performs Exploratory Data Analysis (EDA) on a Superstore sales dataset to uncover business insights related to sales performance, profitability, customer segments, product categories, regional trends, and discount strategies.

The objective is to transform raw business data into actionable insights that can support better decision-making through data cleaning, visualization, and statistical analysis.

🎯 Project Objectives¶

  • Understand the structure and quality of the dataset.
  • Clean and prepare the data for analysis.
  • Analyze sales and profit performance across categories, regions, and customer segments.
  • Identify top-performing and underperforming products.
  • Examine monthly sales and profit trends over time.
  • Analyze the relationship between discounts and profitability.
  • Generate actionable business insights and recommendations.

💼 Business Objective¶

The primary business objective of this analysis is to evaluate the sales and profitability performance of a retail superstore. Through exploratory data analysis, the project aims to identify high-performing products, profitable customer segments, regional sales patterns, seasonal trends, and the impact of discounts on profitability.

The insights generated from this analysis can help business stakeholders make data-driven decisions related to inventory management, pricing strategies, marketing campaigns, and overall business growth.

🛠️ Tools & Technologies Used¶

Tool / Technology Purpose
Python Core programming language used for data analysis
Pandas Data loading, cleaning, transformation, and analysis
NumPy Numerical computations and mathematical operations
Matplotlib Creating static data visualizations
Seaborn Creating statistical and aesthetically enhanced visualizations
Jupyter Notebook (VS Code) Interactive coding, analysis, and project documentation

📂 Dataset¶

Dataset Name: SuperStoreOrders.csv

File Format: CSV

Number of Records: 51,290

Number of Columns: 21

Dataset Description¶

The dataset contains transactional sales records of a retail superstore. Each record represents an individual customer order and includes customer information, product details, order and shipping dates, sales, quantity, discounts, profit, shipping cost, geographical information, and order priority.

This dataset is well suited for Exploratory Data Analysis (EDA) because it enables the analysis of sales performance, profitability, customer behavior, product performance, regional trends, shipping patterns, and time-based business trends.

📥 1. Import Libraries¶

🎯 Purpose¶

Before beginning the analysis, all required Python libraries are imported.

These libraries provide the tools needed for data manipulation, statistical analysis, visualization, and date formatting throughout the project. Importing them at the beginning keeps the notebook organized, avoids redundant code, and follows professional Python coding practices.

In [42]:
# Data Manipulation
import pandas as pd
import numpy as np

# Data Visualization
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import matplotlib.dates as mdates
import seaborn as sns

# Formatting Utilities
from matplotlib.ticker import FuncFormatter

📚 Libraries Used¶

Library Purpose
Pandas (pandas) Used for loading, cleaning, transforming, and analyzing tabular data.
NumPy (numpy) Provides numerical computing functions and supports mathematical operations.
Matplotlib (matplotlib.pyplot) Used to create charts and visualizations throughout the project.
Matplotlib Dates (matplotlib.dates) Provides tools for formatting and displaying date-based axes in time-series visualizations.
Seaborn (seaborn) Used to create statistical visualizations with enhanced aesthetics and simplified plotting functions.
FuncFormatter (matplotlib.ticker.FuncFormatter) Formats chart axes with comma-separated numeric values, improving the readability of large numbers.

💡 Why It Matters¶

Importing all required libraries at the beginning ensures that every subsequent analysis can access the necessary functions without repeatedly importing modules. This approach improves readability, reduces redundancy, and aligns with industry-standard Python development practices.

📂 2. Load the Dataset¶

🎯 Purpose¶

The dataset is loaded into a Pandas DataFrame, which serves as the primary data structure for analysis.

Loading the dataset is the first step in understanding the available data before performing cleaning, exploration, visualization, and business analysis.

In [43]:
df = pd.read_csv("../data/SuperStoreOrders.csv")

🔎 Code Explanation¶

df

  • The variable where the dataset is stored as a Pandas DataFrame.

=

  • Assignment operator. It stores the loaded dataset into the variable df.

pd.read_csv()

  • A Pandas function used to read data from a CSV (Comma-Separated Values) file.

"../data/SuperStoreOrders.csv"

  • Specifies the relative path to the dataset.
  • .. means move one folder up from the current notebook location.
  • data/ is the folder containing the dataset.
  • SuperStoreOrders.csv is the dataset file.

💡 Why It Matters¶

Loading the dataset correctly is the foundation of the entire analysis. Every cleaning step, visualization, statistical calculation, and business insight depends on successfully importing the dataset into a DataFrame.

In [44]:
# Preview the first 5 rows
df.head()
Out[44]:
order_id order_date ship_date ship_mode customer_name segment state country market region ... category sub_category product_name sales quantity discount profit shipping_cost order_priority year

3 IT-2011-3647632 1/1/2011 5/1/2011 Second Class Eugene Moren Home Office Stockholm Sweden EU North ... Office Supplies Paper Enermax Note Cards, Premium 45 3 0.5 -26.055 4.82 High 2011
4 IN-2011-47883 1/1/2011 8/1/2011 Standard Class Joseph Holt Consumer New South Wales Australia APAC Oceania ... Furniture Furnishings Eldon Light Bulb, Duo Pack 114 5 0.1 37.770 4.70 Medium 2011

5 rows × 21 columns

✅ Section Summary¶

The Global Superstore dataset was successfully imported into the notebook and verified using a preview of the first five records. This confirms that the dataset has been loaded correctly and is ready for initial exploration.

🔍 3. Initial Data Exploration¶

🎯 Purpose¶

Before cleaning or analyzing the dataset, it is essential to understand its overall structure and quality.

Initial data exploration helps identify:

  • The size of the dataset.
  • Available columns.
  • Data types.
  • Missing values.
  • Potential data quality issues.

Understanding the dataset at this stage ensures that the subsequent analysis is based on accurate and reliable information.

In [45]:
# Check dataset dimensions
df.shape
Out[45]:
(51290, 21)
In [46]:
# Display all column names
df.columns.tolist()
Out[46]:
['order_id',
 'order_date',
 'ship_date',
 'ship_mode',
 'customer_name',
 'segment',
 'state',
 'country',
 'market',
 'region',
 'product_id',
 'category',
 'sub_category',
 'product_name',
 'sales',
 'quantity',
 'discount',
 'profit',
 'shipping_cost',
 'order_priority',
 'year']
In [47]:
# View data types and missing values
df.info()

<class 'pandas.core.frame.DataFrame'>
RangeIndex: 51290 entries, 0 to 51289
Data columns (total 21 columns):
 #   Column          Non-Null Count  Dtype  
---  ------          --------------  -----  
 0   order_id        51290 non-null  object 
 1   order_date      51290 non-null  object 
 2   ship_date       51290 non-null  object 
 3   ship_mode       51290 non-null  object 
 4   customer_name   51290 non-null  object 
 5   segment         51290 non-null  object 
 6   state           51290 non-null  object 
 7   country         51290 non-null  object 
 8   market          51290 non-null  object 
 9   region          51290 non-null  object 
 10  product_id      51290 non-null  object 
 11  category        51290 non-null  object 
 12  sub_category    51290 non-null  object 
 13  product_name    51290 non-null  object 
 14  sales           51290 non-null  object 
 15  quantity        51290 non-null  int64  
 16  discount        51290 non-null  float64
 17  profit          51290 non-null  float64
 18  shipping_cost   51290 non-null  float64
 19  order_priority  51290 non-null  object 
 20  year            51290 non-null  int64  
dtypes: float64(3), int64(2), object(16)
memory usage: 8.2+ MB

🔎 Code Explanation¶

The following commands were used to understand the overall structure and quality of the dataset before performing data cleaning and analysis.

df.shape¶

  • Returns the dimensions of the DataFrame as a tuple.
  • The first value represents the number of rows (records).
  • The second value represents the number of columns (features).

df.columns.tolist()¶

  • Returns all column names in the DataFrame.
  • Converts the column index into a standard Python list for easier readability.
  • Helps identify the available features for analysis.

df.info()¶

  • Displays a concise summary of the DataFrame.
  • Provides information about:
    • Total number of rows and columns
    • Column names
    • Data types
    • Non-null values
    • Memory usage

This command is essential for understanding the dataset's structure before beginning the data cleaning process.

💡 Why It Matters¶

Initial data exploration provides a high-level understanding of the dataset before any modifications are made.

By examining the dataset dimensions, available columns, and data types, we can identify potential data quality issues, verify that the dataset has been loaded correctly, and determine the preprocessing steps required before conducting Exploratory Data Analysis (EDA).

This step forms the foundation for accurate and reliable business analysis.

🔎 Key Findings¶

Based on the initial exploration of the dataset:

  • The dataset contains 51,290 records and 21 columns.
  • No missing values were detected in any column.
  • Most categorical variables are stored as the object data type.
  • The quantity and year columns are stored as integer (int64) data types.
  • The discount, profit, and shipping_cost columns are stored as floating-point (float64) data types.
  • The sales column is currently stored as an object data type and will require conversion to a numeric data type.
  • The order_date and ship_date columns are currently stored as object data types and will need to be converted to the datetime format during the data cleaning phase.

✅ Section Summary¶

✔️ Dataset dimensions verified.

✔️ Column names reviewed.

✔️ Data types inspected.

✔️ Missing values checked.

✔️ Initial data quality issues identified.

The dataset structure has now been fully understood and is ready for the data cleaning phase.

🧹 4. Data Cleaning¶

🎯 Purpose¶

Raw datasets often contain inconsistencies such as duplicate records, incorrect data types, or formatting issues that can affect the accuracy of the analysis.

The objective of this phase is to identify and resolve data quality issues so that the dataset is accurate, consistent, and ready for reliable exploratory data analysis.

The following data cleaning tasks will be performed:

  • Check for duplicate records.
  • Remove duplicate records (if present).
  • Convert date columns to the appropriate datetime format.
  • Convert numerical columns stored as text into numeric data types.
  • Verify the updated data types after cleaning.

Step 1: Check for Duplicate Records¶

🎯 Step Purpose¶

Before analyzing the dataset, it is important to check whether duplicate records exist.

Duplicate rows can lead to inaccurate calculations, biased results, and misleading business insights. Therefore, identifying and handling duplicate records is an essential part of the data cleaning process.

In [48]:
df.duplicated().sum()
Out[48]:
np.int64(0)

🔎 Code Explanation¶

df.duplicated()

  • Compares each row with all previous rows to determine whether an identical record already exists.
  • Returns a Boolean Series where:
    • True indicates a duplicate record.
    • False indicates a unique record.

.sum()

  • Counts the total number of duplicate records by treating True as 1 and False as 0.
  • This provides the total number of duplicate rows present in the dataset.

The command df.duplicated().sum() is a quick and efficient way to identify whether duplicate records exist before performing any data cleaning.

💡 Why It Matters¶

Duplicate records can negatively impact data analysis by artificially increasing sales, profit, order counts, or other business metrics.

Identifying duplicate records before analysis helps ensure that the dataset accurately represents unique business transactions. This improves the reliability of the insights and supports better business decision-making.

📊 Findings¶

The duplicate record check returned 0, indicating that the dataset does not contain any duplicate rows.

Since no duplicate records were identified, no rows needed to be removed, and the dataset can be used for further analysis without modification at this stage.

✅ Step Summary¶

  • ✔️ Checked the dataset for duplicate records.
  • ✔️ No duplicate records were found.
  • ✔️ No rows were removed from the dataset.
  • ✔️ The dataset is ready for the next data cleaning task.

Step 2: Convert Date Columns¶

🎯 Purpose¶

The order_date and ship_date columns are currently stored as text (object) instead of a proper date format.

Converting these columns to the datetime data type enables accurate time-based analysis, such as monthly and yearly sales trends, shipping duration calculations, and other date-related business insights.

Since the dataset contains multiple date formats, appropriate conversion parameters are used to ensure all dates are interpreted correctly.

In [49]:
df["order_date"] = pd.to_datetime(
    df["order_date"],
    format="mixed",
    dayfirst=True
)

df["ship_date"] = pd.to_datetime(
    df["ship_date"],
    format="mixed",
    dayfirst=True
)
In [50]:
df[["order_date", "ship_date"]].dtypes
Out[50]:
order_date    datetime64[ns]
ship_date     datetime64[ns]
dtype: object

🔎 Code Explanation¶

The dtypes attribute is used to display the data type of one or more columns in a DataFrame.

In this step, it is used to verify whether the order_date and ship_date columns have been successfully converted to the datetime64[ns] data type.

Verifying the data types after conversion is an important validation step that ensures the previous data cleaning operation was completed successfully.

💡 Why It Matters¶

Verifying data types is an essential part of the data cleaning process.

If date columns remain stored as text (object), time-based analyses such as monthly sales trends, yearly performance, shipping duration calculations, and seasonal analysis cannot be performed correctly.

Confirming that both columns are stored as datetime64[ns] ensures the dataset is ready for accurate time-series analysis.

📊 Key Findings¶

The verification confirms that both order_date and ship_date have been successfully converted to the datetime64[ns] data type.

The dataset contains multiple date formats (e.g., 1/1/2011 and 17-09-2012). Using format="mixed" with dayfirst=True ensures that all dates are interpreted correctly during conversion.

The date columns are now properly formatted and ready for further analysis.

✅ Step Summary¶

  • ✔️ Converted order_date to datetime format.
  • ✔️ Converted ship_date to datetime format.
  • ✔️ Successfully handled mixed date formats.
  • ✔️ Verified the updated data types.
  • ✔️ The dataset is now ready for time-based analysis.

Step 3: Convert Sales Column to Numeric¶

🎯 Step Purpose¶

The sales column represents the revenue generated from each transaction. Although it contains numerical values, it is currently stored as the object data type.

Before performing calculations such as total sales, average sales, profit analysis, and visualizations, the column must be converted into a numeric data type.

Converting the column ensures that mathematical operations can be performed accurately throughout the analysis.

In [51]:
# Remove commas from the Sales values
df["sales"] = df["sales"].str.replace(",", "", regex=False)

# Convert the column to numeric
df["sales"] = pd.to_numeric(df["sales"])

# Verify the updated data type
df[["sales"]].dtypes
Out[51]:
sales    int64
dtype: object

🔎 Code Explanation¶

The sales column was originally stored as the object data type because some values contained commas as thousands separators (for example, 1,648).

To prepare the column for numerical analysis:

  • The str.replace() method removes commas from each value, converting them into plain numeric strings.
  • The pd.to_numeric() function converts the cleaned text values into the int64 numeric data type.
  • Finally, the dtypes attribute is used to verify that the conversion was successful.

This process ensures that the sales column can be used correctly in mathematical calculations, statistical analysis, and data visualizations.

💡 Why It Matters¶

Numerical analysis cannot be performed accurately when numeric values are stored as text.

Converting the sales column to a numeric data type enables calculations such as:

  • Total Sales
  • Average Sales
  • Sales Distribution
  • Correlation Analysis
  • Business Performance Metrics
  • Data Visualizations

This conversion ensures that all mathematical operations produce accurate and reliable results.

📈 Key Observations¶

The sales column was initially stored as the object data type due to the presence of commas in some values (for example, 1,648).

After removing the commas and converting the column using pd.to_numeric(), the data type was successfully changed to int64.

The column is now fully prepared for numerical analysis and visualization.

✅ Step Summary¶

  • ✔️ Identified formatting issues in the sales column.
  • ✔️ Removed commas from sales values.
  • ✔️ Converted the sales column to the int64 data type.
  • ✔️ Verified the updated data type.
  • ✔️ The sales column is now ready for numerical analysis.

Step 4: Verify Data Types¶

🎯 Step Purpose¶

After completing all data cleaning operations, it is important to verify that each column has the appropriate data type.

Correct data types are essential because they enable accurate calculations, proper date operations, meaningful statistical analysis, and reliable visualizations throughout the project.

This final verification confirms that the dataset is fully prepared for Exploratory Data Analysis (EDA).

In [52]:
df.dtypes
Out[52]:
order_id                  object
order_date        datetime64[ns]
ship_date         datetime64[ns]
ship_mode                 object
customer_name             object
segment                   object
state                     object
country                   object
market                    object
region                    object
product_id                object
category                  object
sub_category              object
product_name              object
sales                      int64
quantity                   int64
discount                 float64
profit                   float64
shipping_cost            float64
order_priority            object
year                       int64
dtype: object

🔎 Code Explanation¶

The dtypes attribute is used to display the data type of every column in the DataFrame.

After completing all data cleaning operations, this step verifies that important columns such as order_date, ship_date, and sales have been successfully converted to their appropriate data types.

This serves as the final validation before beginning Exploratory Data Analysis (EDA).

💡 Why It Matters¶

Verifying data types is a crucial quality assurance step in the data cleaning process.

Correct data types ensure that:

  • Date columns support time-based analysis.
  • Numeric columns allow mathematical calculations.
  • Statistical analysis produces accurate results.
  • Visualizations are generated correctly.

Without proper data types, business analysis may produce incorrect or misleading insights.

📈 Observations¶

The verification confirms that all major analytical columns now have the correct data types.

  • order_date and ship_date are stored as datetime64[ns].
  • sales and quantity are stored as int64.
  • discount, profit, and shipping_cost are stored as float64.

The dataset is now fully prepared for reliable exploratory data analysis.

✅ Step Summary¶

  • ✔️ Verified the data types of all columns.
  • ✔️ Confirmed successful date conversion.
  • ✔️ Confirmed successful numeric conversion.
  • ✔️ Validated the dataset for further analysis.
  • ✔️ Data cleaning process successfully completed.

📋 Data Cleaning Checklist¶

Cleaning Task Status
Duplicate records checked ✅ Completed
Duplicate records removed (if required) ✅ Not Required
Date columns converted ✅ Completed
Sales column converted to numeric ✅ Completed
Data types verified ✅ Completed
Dataset ready for analysis ✅ Completed

🏁 Data Cleaning Conclusion¶

All essential data cleaning tasks have been successfully completed.

The dataset was examined for duplicate records, inconsistent date formats, incorrect numerical formatting, and data type issues. Appropriate preprocessing techniques were applied to ensure the data is clean, consistent, and analysis-ready.

With the data cleaning phase complete, the dataset is now fully prepared for Exploratory Data Analysis (EDA), where meaningful business insights will be extracted through statistical analysis and data visualization.

📊 5. Exploratory Data Analysis (EDA)¶

🎯 Purpose¶

Exploratory Data Analysis (EDA) is the process of examining, summarizing, and visualizing data to uncover meaningful patterns, trends, relationships, and anomalies.

The objective of this section is to analyze the Superstore dataset from a business perspective by answering key business questions related to sales performance, profitability, customer behavior, product performance, regional trends, and operational efficiency.

The insights obtained from this analysis will support data-driven decision-making and help identify opportunities for improving overall business performance.

📌 5.1 Overall Business Performance (KPI Analysis)¶

📖 Business Scenario¶

Before analyzing individual business dimensions such as product categories, customer segments, and regions, it is important to understand the company's overall business performance.

Key Performance Indicators (KPIs) provide a high-level summary of the organization's financial performance and operational scale. These metrics help answer fundamental business questions, including:

  • How much revenue has the company generated?
  • How profitable is the business?
  • How many customers have placed orders?
  • What is the average value generated per transaction?

This section establishes the overall business context and serves as the foundation for all subsequent analyses in this notebook.

🎯 Business Questions¶

This analysis aims to answer the following business questions:

  1. What is the company's total sales revenue?

  2. What is the company's total profit?

  3. How many unique orders have been placed?

  4. How many unique customers have made purchases?

  5. What is the average sales value per order?

  6. What is the average profit earned per order?

🎯 Overall Step Purpose¶

The objective of this section is to calculate the key performance indicators (KPIs) that summarize the company's overall business performance.

These KPIs provide a concise overview of the organization's revenue, profitability, customer base, and order volume. Understanding these metrics establishes a benchmark against which all subsequent analyses can be interpreted.

📊 KPI Summary¶

The following table summarizes the six key performance indicators (KPIs) calculated from the Global Superstore dataset. These metrics provide a concise overview of the company's overall business performance and serve as the foundation for the detailed analyses presented in the subsequent sections.

In [53]:
total_sales = df["sales"].sum()

total_profit = df["profit"].sum()

total_orders = df["order_id"].nunique()

total_customers = df["customer_name"].nunique()

average_sales = df["sales"].mean()

average_profit = df["profit"].mean()
In [54]:
kpi_summary = pd.DataFrame({
    "KPI": [
        "Total Sales",
        "Total Profit",
        "Total Orders",
        "Total Customers",
        "Average Sales per Order",
        "Average Profit per Order"
    ],
    "Value": [
        df["sales"].sum(),
        df["profit"].sum(),
        df["order_id"].nunique(),
        df["customer_name"].nunique(),
        df["sales"].mean(),
        df["profit"].mean()
    ]
})

kpi_summary.style.hide(axis="index").format({
    "Value": lambda x: f"{x:,.2f}" if isinstance(x, float) else f"{x:,}"
})
Out[54]:
KPI Value
Total Sales 12,642,905.00
Total Profit 1,469,034.82
Total Orders 25,035.00
Total Customers 795.00
Average Sales per Order 246.50
Average Profit per Order 28.64

📝 KPI Interpretation¶

The KPI summary presents a consolidated overview of the company's overall business performance.

It highlights the organization's revenue, profitability, customer base, and order activity, providing a baseline for evaluating the detailed analyses presented in the subsequent sections of this notebook.

These metrics help quantify the overall scale of the business before examining individual categories, customer segments, markets, and regions.

📌 Executive Snapshot¶

The KPI analysis indicates that the Superstore operated at a substantial business scale, generating ₹12.64 million in total sales and ₹1.47 million in profit during the analysis period. With 25,035 unique orders placed by 795 customers, the business maintained positive overall profitability while achieving consistent sales performance.

Key Highlights¶

  • 💰 Total Sales: ₹12.64 million
  • 📈 Total Profit: ₹1.47 million
  • 🛒 Unique Orders: 25,035
  • 👥 Unique Customers: 795
  • 💵 Average Sales per Order: ₹246.50
  • 📊 Average Profit per Order: ₹28.64

These KPIs establish the baseline for the detailed analyses that follow, including product category performance, customer segments, regional trends, market analysis, and profitability drivers.

🔎 Code Explanation¶

This code calculates six important Key Performance Indicators (KPIs) using built-in Pandas aggregation functions.

  • sum() calculates the total sales and total profit.
  • nunique() counts the number of unique orders and unique customers.
  • mean() calculates the average sales and average profit per order.
  • Python's formatted string literals (f-strings) are used to display the results in a clean and readable format with comma separators and appropriate decimal precision.

💡 Why It Matters¶

Key Performance Indicators (KPIs) provide a quick summary of the overall health of a business.

Before analyzing specific business dimensions, decision-makers need to understand the organization's overall performance. These metrics serve as benchmark values that help compare the contribution of different product categories, customer segments, markets, and regions throughout the remainder of the analysis.

📈 Key Observations¶

  • The Superstore generated 12.64 million USD in total sales during the analysis period.
  • The business earned a total profit of 1.47 million USD.
  • A total of 25,035 unique customer orders were processed.
  • The customer base consisted of 795 unique customers.
  • The average sales value per order was 246.50 USD.
  • The average profit earned per order was 28.64 USD.

🧠 Business Insights¶

The KPI summary indicates that the Superstore operates at a substantial business scale, generating strong overall revenue while maintaining positive profitability.

Although the business is profitable overall, the average profit earned per order is relatively modest compared to the average sales value. This suggests that profitability may vary significantly across different products, customer segments, and regions.

The following analyses will investigate these business dimensions to identify the primary drivers of revenue and profit, as well as areas where operational improvements can be made.

💼 Business Recommendation¶

Management should continue monitoring these KPIs as primary performance indicators while investigating the factors that influence profitability across different business dimensions.

Future business strategies should focus on increasing average profit per order through optimized pricing, product mix, inventory management, and targeted marketing initiatives.

⭐ Key Takeaway¶

The Superstore generated strong overall revenue and remained profitable, providing a solid foundation for deeper business performance analysis.

✅ Section Summary (5.1)¶

This section evaluated the Superstore's overall business performance using six key performance indicators (KPIs), providing a comprehensive overview of the company's revenue, profitability, customer base, and order activity.

The analysis established the overall business baseline and confirmed that the company operated profitably during the analysis period. These benchmark metrics will serve as reference points for interpreting the detailed analyses of product categories, customer segments, regional performance, markets, and other business dimensions presented in the following sections.

📌 Next Analysis¶

The KPI analysis provided a high-level overview of the company's overall business performance by establishing key benchmark metrics for sales, profitability, customer activity, and order volume.

To better understand the factors driving these overall results, the next section examines business performance across different product categories. This analysis will identify the categories that contribute most significantly to revenue and profit, helping management recognize both the company's strongest-performing products and areas with opportunities for improvement.

📊 5.2 Category Analysis¶

📖 Business Scenario¶

Product categories play a critical role in determining a company's overall business performance. Each category contributes differently to revenue generation and profitability, making it essential to evaluate their individual performance.

Understanding how each category performs enables management to identify the organization's strongest revenue drivers, recognize underperforming areas, and make informed decisions regarding inventory planning, pricing strategies, marketing investments, and resource allocation.

This analysis focuses on comparing the sales and profit generated by each product category to uncover meaningful business insights.

🎯 Business Questions¶

This analysis aims to answer the following business questions:

  1. Which product category generates the highest sales?

  2. Which product category contributes the highest profit?

  3. Do higher sales always result in higher profitability?

  4. Which product category demonstrates the strongest overall business performance?

  5. Which category requires greater managerial attention to improve profitability?

🎯 Overall Step Purpose¶

The objective of this section is to evaluate the business performance of each product category using two key performance metrics: Total Sales and Total Profit.

The analysis begins with a high-level comparison of category performance through a summary table, followed by separate visual analyses of sales and profitability. This approach helps identify the categories that contribute most significantly to the company's revenue and profit while highlighting areas that may require strategic improvement.

📋 Category Performance Summary¶

The following table summarizes the overall performance of each product category based on two key business metrics: Total Sales and Total Profit.

This high-level comparison provides an overview of how each category contributes to the company's revenue and profitability before examining sales and profit individually through visual analysis.

In [55]:
category_summary = (
    df.groupby("category")
      .agg(
          Total_Sales=("sales", "sum"),
          Total_Profit=("profit", "sum")
      )
      .round(2)
)
In [56]:
category_summary.style.format({
    "Total_Sales": "{:,.0f}",
    "Total_Profit": "{:,.2f}"
})
Out[56]:
  Total_Sales Total_Profit
category    
Furniture 4,110,884 286,782.25
Office Supplies 3,787,330 518,473.83
Technology 4,744,691 663,778.73

📝 Summary Table Interpretation¶

The summary table reveals noticeable differences in the business performance of the three product categories.

Technology generated the highest sales as well as the highest profit, indicating strong overall performance. Furniture also contributed substantially to total sales but produced comparatively lower profit, suggesting relatively lower profit margins. Office Supplies generated the lowest sales among the three categories while maintaining healthy profitability.

These findings provide an initial understanding of category performance and will be explored further through separate analyses of sales and profit.

📊 5.2.1 Sales Analysis by Category¶

🎯 Step Purpose¶

The objective of this analysis is to compare the total sales generated by each product category.

By visualizing category-wise sales, we can identify the primary revenue drivers of the business and understand how sales are distributed across different product categories.

📊 Chart Introduction¶

The following bar chart illustrates the total sales generated by each product category.

Visualizing the sales distribution makes it easier to compare category performance and quickly identify which categories contribute the most to the company's overall revenue.

In [57]:
plt.figure(figsize=(8,5))

plt.bar(
    category_summary.index,
    category_summary["Total_Sales"]
)

plt.title("Category-wise Total Sales", fontsize=14, fontweight="bold")

plt.xlabel("Category", fontsize=11)

plt.ylabel("Total Sales (USD)", fontsize=11)

plt.grid(axis="y", linestyle="--", alpha=0.3)

plt.gca().yaxis.set_major_formatter(
    FuncFormatter(lambda x, pos: f'{x:,.0f}')
)

plt.tight_layout()

plt.show()

🔎 Code Explanation¶

This visualization is created using Matplotlib's bar() function to compare the total sales generated by each product category.

The chart uses the precomputed category_summary table, where:

  • The x-axis represents the product categories.
  • The y-axis represents the total sales generated by each category.

Additional formatting such as chart title, axis labels, gridlines, and comma-separated number formatting improves readability and makes the visualization suitable for business reporting.

💡 Why It Matters¶

Sales performance indicates how much revenue each product category contributes to the business.

Comparing category-wise sales helps management identify the organization's primary revenue sources, evaluate product demand, and prioritize future investments in inventory, marketing, and product development.

📈 Key Observations¶

  • Technology generated the highest total sales among all product categories.
  • Furniture recorded the second-highest sales performance.
  • Office Supplies generated the lowest total sales.
  • Although the differences are not extremely large, Technology clearly outperformed the other two categories in terms of revenue generation.

🧠 Business Insights¶

The Technology category serves as the company's strongest revenue driver, indicating high customer demand and strong market performance.

Furniture also contributes significantly to overall revenue, while Office Supplies generates comparatively lower sales. However, high sales alone do not necessarily imply higher profitability. Therefore, the next analysis examines category-wise profit to determine whether revenue generation translates into financial performance.

💼 Business Recommendation¶

Management should continue strengthening the Technology category through strategic marketing, product expansion, and inventory optimization to sustain its revenue leadership.

At the same time, further investigation should be conducted to understand the factors limiting sales growth in the Office Supplies category and identify opportunities for improvement.

⭐ Key Takeaway¶

Technology is the highest revenue-generating product category and serves as the primary sales driver of the business.

✅ Step Summary¶

This analysis compared the total sales generated by each product category and identified Technology as the leading contributor to overall revenue. The findings provide valuable insight into the company's sales distribution and establish the foundation for evaluating whether high sales also translate into high profitability in the next analysis.

📊 5.2.2 Profit Analysis by Category¶

🎯 Step Purpose¶

The objective of this analysis is to compare the total profit generated by each product category.

While sales indicate revenue generation, profit provides a clearer measure of financial performance. This analysis helps identify which product categories contribute most effectively to the company's profitability.

📊 Chart Introduction¶

The following bar chart illustrates the total profit earned by each product category.

By comparing category-wise profit, we can determine which categories generate the greatest financial returns and assess whether high sales are accompanied by strong profitability.

In [58]:
plt.figure(figsize=(8,5))

plt.bar(
    category_summary.index,
    category_summary["Total_Profit"]
)

plt.title(
    "Category-wise Total Profit",
    fontsize=14,
    fontweight="bold"
)

plt.xlabel("Category", fontsize=11)

plt.ylabel("Total Profit (USD)", fontsize=11)

plt.grid(axis="y", linestyle="--", alpha=0.3)

plt.gca().yaxis.set_major_formatter(
    FuncFormatter(lambda x, pos: f"{x:,.0f}")
)

plt.tight_layout()

plt.show()

🔎 Code Explanation¶

This visualization is created using Matplotlib's bar() function to compare the total profit generated by each product category.

The chart is based on the precomputed category_summary table, where:

  • The x-axis represents the product categories.
  • The y-axis represents the total profit earned by each category.

Formatting elements such as descriptive axis labels, comma-separated number formatting, gridlines, and a clear chart title improve readability and make the visualization suitable for business reporting.

💡 Why It Matters¶

Profitability is a key indicator of business success because it reflects the company's ability to generate financial returns from its operations.

Comparing profit across product categories helps identify the areas that create the greatest business value, supports strategic decision-making, and enables management to allocate resources more effectively.

📈 Key Observations¶

  • Technology generated the highest total profit among all product categories.
  • Office Supplies ranked second in terms of total profit.
  • Furniture generated the lowest total profit.
  • Furniture produced lower profit than Office Supplies despite generating higher total sales.

💼 Business Recommendation¶

Management should continue strengthening the Technology category through product expansion, targeted marketing initiatives, and efficient inventory management to sustain its strong financial performance.

For the Furniture category, a detailed profitability analysis should be conducted to identify the factors reducing profit margins. Reviewing pricing strategies, discount policies, procurement costs, and operational expenses may help improve profitability while maintaining sales performance.

⭐ Key Takeaway¶

Technology is the strongest-performing product category, while Furniture presents the greatest opportunity for improving profitability.

✅ Analysis Summary¶

This analysis compared the total profit generated by each product category and identified Technology as the company's most profitable category. It also revealed that strong sales performance does not always result in high profitability, emphasizing the importance of evaluating both revenue and profit when assessing business performance.

✅ Section Summary (5.2)¶

This section evaluated the performance of the company's product categories using Total Sales and Total Profit as the primary business metrics.

The analysis identified Technology as the strongest-performing category, leading in both revenue and profitability. It also revealed that high sales do not always translate into high profit, as the Furniture category generated substantial sales but comparatively lower profit than Office Supplies.

These findings highlight the importance of evaluating both sales and profitability when assessing product performance and provide valuable insights for product portfolio management, pricing strategies, and future business planning.

➡️ Next Analysis¶

The category analysis identified the products that contribute most significantly to the company's sales and profitability. However, business performance is influenced not only by the products being sold but also by the customers purchasing them.

The next section shifts the focus from products to customers by examining the performance of different customer segments. This analysis will help determine which customer groups generate the greatest business value and provide insights for developing more targeted marketing and sales strategies.

📊 5.3 Customer Segment Analysis¶

📖 Business Scenario¶

Customers are one of the most valuable assets of any business. However, not all customer groups contribute equally to revenue and profitability. Understanding the purchasing behavior and financial contribution of different customer segments enables organizations to develop targeted marketing strategies, improve customer relationships, and allocate resources more effectively.

The Global Superstore categorizes its customers into three segments: Consumer, Corporate, and Home Office. Analyzing the performance of these segments helps identify the most valuable customer groups and supports data-driven business decisions aimed at maximizing long-term profitability.

🎯 Business Questions¶

This analysis aims to answer the following business questions:

  1. Which customer segment generates the highest sales?

  2. Which customer segment contributes the highest profit?

  3. Does the most profitable segment also generate the highest sales?

  4. Which customer segment creates the greatest overall business value?

  5. Which customer segment offers opportunities for future business growth?

🎯 Overall Step Purpose¶

The objective of this section is to evaluate the performance of different customer segments using two key business metrics: Total Sales and Total Profit.

The analysis begins with a summary comparison of all customer segments, followed by separate visual analyses of sales and profitability. This approach helps identify the customer groups that contribute most significantly to the company's financial performance and supports the development of effective customer-focused business strategies.

📋 Customer Segment Performance Summary¶

The following table summarizes the overall performance of each customer segment based on two key business metrics: Total Sales and Total Profit.

This high-level comparison provides an overview of how each customer segment contributes to the company's revenue and profitability before examining sales and profit individually through visual analysis.

In [59]:
segment_summary = (
    df.groupby("segment")
      .agg(
          Total_Sales=("sales", "sum"),
          Total_Profit=("profit", "sum")
      )
      .round(2)
)

segment_summary.style.format({
    "Total_Sales": "{:,.0f}",
    "Total_Profit": "{:,.2f}"
})
Out[59]:
  Total_Sales Total_Profit
segment    
Consumer 6,508,141 749,239.78
Corporate 3,824,808 442,785.86
Home Office 2,309,956 277,009.18

📝 Summary Table Interpretation¶

The summary table indicates clear differences in the financial performance of the three customer segments.

The Consumer segment generated the highest sales and the highest profit, making it the company's strongest-performing customer group. The Corporate segment ranked second in both sales and profitability, while the Home Office segment contributed the lowest sales and profit.

These findings provide an overall understanding of customer segment performance and establish the foundation for the detailed sales and profit analyses presented in the following subsections.

📊 5.3.1 Sales Analysis by Customer Segment¶

🎯 Step Purpose¶

The objective of this analysis is to compare the total sales generated by each customer segment.

By visualizing segment-wise sales, we can identify the customer groups that contribute the most to the company's revenue and better understand the distribution of sales across different customer segments.

📊 Chart Introduction¶

The following bar chart illustrates the total sales generated by each customer segment.

Visualizing the sales distribution enables an easy comparison of customer segment performance and helps identify the organization's primary revenue-generating customer groups.

In [19]:
plt.figure(figsize=(8,5))

plt.bar(
    segment_summary.index,
    segment_summary["Total_Sales"]
)

plt.title(
    "Segment-wise Total Sales",
    fontsize=14,
    fontweight="bold"
)

plt.xlabel("Customer Segment", fontsize=11)

plt.ylabel("Total Sales (USD)", fontsize=11)

plt.grid(axis="y", linestyle="--", alpha=0.3)

plt.gca().yaxis.set_major_formatter(
    FuncFormatter(lambda x, pos: f"{x:,.0f}")
)

plt.tight_layout()

plt.show()

🔎 Code Explanation¶

This visualization is created using Matplotlib's bar() function to compare the total sales generated by each customer segment.

The chart is based on the precomputed segment_summary table, where:

  • The x-axis represents the customer segments.
  • The y-axis represents the total sales generated by each segment.

Additional formatting, including descriptive axis labels, comma-separated number formatting, gridlines, and a clear chart title, enhances readability and makes the visualization suitable for business reporting.

💡 Why It Matters¶

Customer segments contribute differently to business revenue. Understanding their sales performance helps management identify the organization's most valuable customer groups and develop targeted marketing, sales, and customer retention strategies.

Segment-wise sales analysis also supports better resource allocation by highlighting where future business growth opportunities are most likely to exist.

📈 Key Observations¶

  • The Consumer segment generated the highest total sales among all customer segments.
  • The Corporate segment ranked second in terms of total sales.
  • The Home Office segment generated the lowest total sales.
  • The Consumer segment contributed substantially more revenue than both the Corporate and Home Office segments.

🧠 Business Insights¶

The Consumer segment is the company's primary revenue-generating customer group, indicating strong customer demand and a broad purchasing base within this segment.

Although the Corporate and Home Office segments contribute a smaller share of total sales, they may represent opportunities for targeted business expansion. Understanding their purchasing behavior could help the company design more effective marketing campaigns and customer engagement strategies.

💼 Business Recommendation¶

Management should continue strengthening the Consumer segment through customer loyalty programs, personalized promotions, and improved customer experience to maintain its strong revenue contribution.

For the Corporate and Home Office segments, targeted marketing initiatives and customized product offerings should be explored to increase customer acquisition and encourage higher purchasing activity.

⭐ Key Takeaway¶

The Consumer segment is the company's largest revenue-generating customer segment and represents its strongest source of sales.

✅ Step Summary¶

This analysis compared the total sales generated by each customer segment and identified the Consumer segment as the company's primary revenue contributor. The findings provide valuable insight into customer purchasing patterns and establish the foundation for evaluating whether the highest-selling segment also delivers the greatest profitability.

📊 5.3.2 Profit Analysis by Customer Segment¶

🎯 Step Purpose¶

The objective of this analysis is to compare the total profit generated by each customer segment.

While sales measure revenue generation, profit reflects the actual financial value contributed by each customer segment. This analysis helps identify the customer groups that contribute most effectively to the company's overall profitability.

📊 Chart Introduction¶

The following bar chart illustrates the total profit generated by each customer segment.

By comparing segment-wise profit, we can identify the customer groups that create the greatest financial value and evaluate whether the highest-selling segments also generate the highest profitability.

In [20]:
plt.figure(figsize=(8,5))

plt.bar(
    segment_summary.index,
    segment_summary["Total_Profit"]
)

plt.title(
    "Segment-wise Total Profit",
    fontsize=14,
    fontweight="bold"
)

plt.xlabel("Customer Segment", fontsize=11)

plt.ylabel("Total Profit (USD)", fontsize=11)

plt.grid(axis="y", linestyle="--", alpha=0.3)

plt.gca().yaxis.set_major_formatter(
    FuncFormatter(lambda x, pos: f"{x:,.0f}")
)

plt.tight_layout()

plt.show()

🔎 Code Explanation¶

This visualization is created using Matplotlib's bar() function to compare the total profit generated by each customer segment.

The chart is based on the precomputed segment_summary table, where:

  • The x-axis represents the customer segments.
  • The y-axis represents the total profit generated by each segment.

Additional formatting, including descriptive axis labels, comma-separated number formatting, gridlines, and a clear chart title, enhances readability and makes the visualization suitable for business reporting.

💡 Why It Matters¶

Profitability is a key indicator of customer value because it reflects the financial return generated by each customer segment.

Analyzing segment-wise profit helps management identify the customer groups that contribute most effectively to business growth and supports informed decisions related to customer acquisition, retention, and resource allocation.

📈 Key Observations¶

  • The Consumer segment generated the highest total profit among all customer segments.
  • The Corporate segment ranked second in terms of total profit.
  • The Home Office segment generated the lowest total profit.
  • The ranking of customer segments remained consistent across both sales and profit.

🧠 Business Insights¶

The Consumer segment is the company's most valuable customer group, contributing the highest revenue as well as the highest profit. This indicates that the segment not only purchases frequently but also creates strong financial value for the business.

The Corporate segment demonstrates solid business potential and may offer opportunities for further growth through targeted relationship management and customized business solutions. Although the Home Office segment contributes the least to overall profitability, it may still represent a niche market with potential for expansion through specialized marketing strategies.

💼 Business Recommendation¶

Management should continue strengthening customer engagement within the Consumer segment through loyalty programs, personalized marketing, and enhanced customer experience initiatives.

For the Corporate and Home Office segments, focused marketing campaigns, tailored product offerings, and customer relationship strategies should be explored to increase profitability and expand their contribution to overall business performance.

⭐ Key Takeaway¶

The Consumer segment is the company's most profitable customer segment and represents its highest overall business value.

✅ Analysis Summary¶

This analysis compared the total profit generated by each customer segment and identified the Consumer segment as the company's strongest contributor to profitability. The results demonstrate that the segment leading in sales also leads in profit, confirming its importance to the organization's overall financial performance.

✅ Section Summary (5.3)¶

This section evaluated the performance of the company's customer segments using total sales and total profit as key performance indicators. The analysis identified the Consumer segment as the strongest-performing customer group, contributing the highest revenue as well as the highest profit.

These findings highlight the importance of understanding customer segment performance when developing marketing strategies, strengthening customer relationships, and allocating business resources effectively.

➡️ Next Analysis¶

The customer segment analysis identified the groups that contribute most significantly to the company's sales and profitability. However, customer performance can also vary across different geographical locations.

The next section shifts the focus from customer segments to regions by examining regional sales and profit performance. This analysis will help identify the company's strongest geographic markets and highlight regions that offer opportunities for future business growth.

📊 5.4 Regional Analysis¶

📖 Business Scenario¶

Geographical performance plays a critical role in business decision-making. Sales and profitability often vary across regions due to differences in customer demand, market size, competition, economic conditions, and operational efficiency.

By analyzing regional performance, organizations can identify their strongest markets, recognize underperforming regions, and make informed decisions regarding resource allocation, market expansion, and regional business strategies.

This analysis evaluates the performance of each region using Total Sales and Total Profit to determine how different geographical areas contribute to the company's overall business performance.

🎯 Business Questions¶

This analysis aims to answer the following business questions:

  1. Which region generates the highest sales?

  2. Which region contributes the highest profit?

  3. Are high-sales regions also the most profitable?

  4. Which regions require greater business attention and improvement?

  5. How can regional performance support future business expansion strategies?

🎯 Overall Step Purpose¶

The objective of this section is to evaluate the performance of different geographical regions using Total Sales and Total Profit.

The analysis begins with a summary comparison of regional performance, followed by separate visual analyses of sales and profitability. The findings will help identify the company's strongest geographical markets and support data-driven decisions related to regional growth and business planning.

📋 Regional Performance Summary¶

The following table summarizes the performance of each geographical region using Total Sales and Total Profit.

This high-level comparison provides an overview of how different regions contribute to the company's overall revenue and profitability before examining sales and profit individually through visual analysis.

In [21]:
region_summary = (
    df.groupby("region")
      .agg(
          Total_Sales=("sales", "sum"),
          Total_Profit=("profit", "sum")
      )
      .round(2)
)

region_summary.style.format({
    "Total_Sales": "{:,.0f}",
    "Total_Profit": "{:,.2f}"
})
Out[21]:
  Total_Sales Total_Profit
region    
Africa 783,776 88,871.63
Canada 66,932 17,817.39
Caribbean 324,281 34,571.32
Central 2,822,399 311,403.98
Central Asia 752,839 132,480.19
EMEA 806,184 43,897.97
East 678,834 91,522.78
North 1,248,192 194,597.95
North Asia 848,349 165,578.42
Oceania 1,100,207 121,666.64
South 1,600,960 140,355.77
Southeast Asia 884,438 17,852.33
West 725,514 108,418.45

📝 Summary Table Interpretation¶

The summary table shows noticeable differences in business performance across the company's geographical regions.

The Central region generated the highest sales as well as the highest profit, making it the company's strongest-performing region. In contrast, some regions with relatively high sales, such as South and Southeast Asia, generated comparatively lower profit, indicating that higher revenue does not always result in greater profitability.

These findings provide an overall understanding of regional performance and establish the foundation for the detailed sales and profit analyses presented in the following subsections.

📊 5.4.1 Sales Analysis by Region¶

🎯 Step Purpose¶

The objective of this analysis is to compare the total sales generated across different geographical regions.

By visualizing regional sales performance, we can identify the company's strongest revenue-generating markets and better understand the distribution of sales across different geographical areas.

📊 Chart Introduction¶

The following bar chart illustrates the total sales generated by each geographical region.

Visualizing regional sales performance enables an easy comparison of business activity across markets and helps identify the regions that contribute most significantly to the company's overall revenue.

In [22]:
plt.figure(figsize=(11,6))

plt.bar(
    region_summary.index,
    region_summary["Total_Sales"]
)

plt.title(
    "Region-wise Total Sales",
    fontsize=14,
    fontweight="bold"
)

plt.xlabel("Region", fontsize=11)

plt.ylabel("Total Sales (USD)", fontsize=11)

plt.xticks(rotation=45, ha="right")

plt.grid(axis="y", linestyle="--", alpha=0.3)

plt.gca().yaxis.set_major_formatter(
    FuncFormatter(lambda x, pos: f"{x:,.0f}")
)

plt.tight_layout()

plt.show()

🔎 Code Explanation¶

This visualization is created using Matplotlib's bar() function to compare the total sales generated by each geographical region.

The chart is based on the precomputed region_summary table, where:

  • The x-axis represents the geographical regions.
  • The y-axis represents the total sales generated by each region.

Additional formatting, including descriptive axis labels, rotated region names, comma-separated number formatting, gridlines, and a clear chart title, enhances readability and makes the visualization suitable for business reporting.

💡 Why It Matters¶

Regional sales analysis helps organizations understand which geographical markets contribute most significantly to overall revenue. Identifying high-performing and low-performing regions enables management to allocate resources more effectively, strengthen successful markets, and develop improvement strategies for underperforming regions.

Understanding regional sales performance also supports business expansion, market prioritization, and strategic decision-making.

📈 Key Observations¶

  • The Central region generated the highest total sales among all regions.
  • The South region ranked second in terms of total sales.
  • The North region recorded the third-highest sales.
  • Canada generated the lowest total sales.
  • Considerable variation exists in sales performance across different geographical regions.

🧠 Business Insights¶

The Central region represents the company's strongest revenue-generating market, indicating high customer demand and strong business presence in this region.

The substantial differences in regional sales suggest that market potential varies considerably across geographical locations. Regions with relatively lower sales may require targeted marketing efforts, improved distribution strategies, or deeper market analysis to identify opportunities for growth.

💼 Business Recommendation¶

Management should continue investing in high-performing regions such as Central and South to sustain their strong sales performance while identifying the key success factors that can be replicated in other regions.

For lower-performing regions, particularly Canada and the Caribbean, management should investigate potential market barriers and consider targeted promotional campaigns, customer acquisition strategies, or operational improvements to stimulate sales growth.

⭐ Key Takeaway¶

The Central region is the company's largest revenue-generating market, while significant differences in regional sales indicate opportunities for targeted market development.

✅ Analysis Summary¶

This analysis compared total sales across geographical regions and identified the Central region as the company's strongest revenue contributor. The results highlight considerable variation in regional sales performance and provide valuable insights for market prioritization and regional business strategy.

📊 5.4.2 Profit Analysis by Region¶

🎯 Step Purpose¶

The objective of this analysis is to compare the total profit generated across different geographical regions.

While sales indicate revenue generation, profit reflects the actual financial contribution of each region. This analysis helps identify the regions that create the greatest business value and supports strategic decisions for regional business growth.

📊 Chart Introduction¶

The following bar chart illustrates the total profit generated by each geographical region.

By comparing regional profitability, we can identify the company's most valuable markets and determine whether the regions with the highest sales also contribute the highest profit.

In [23]:
plt.figure(figsize=(11,6))

plt.bar(
    region_summary.index,
    region_summary["Total_Profit"]
)

plt.title(
    "Region-wise Total Profit",
    fontsize=14,
    fontweight="bold"
)

plt.xlabel("Region", fontsize=11)

plt.ylabel("Total Profit (USD)", fontsize=11)

plt.xticks(rotation=45, ha="right")

plt.grid(axis="y", linestyle="--", alpha=0.3)

plt.gca().yaxis.set_major_formatter(
    FuncFormatter(lambda x, pos: f"{x:,.0f}")
)

plt.tight_layout()

plt.show()

🔎 Code Explanation¶

This visualization is created using Matplotlib's bar() function to compare the total profit generated by each geographical region.

The chart is based on the precomputed region_summary table, where:

  • The x-axis represents the geographical regions.
  • The y-axis represents the total profit generated by each region.

Additional formatting, including descriptive axis labels, rotated region names, comma-separated number formatting, gridlines, and a clear chart title, improves readability and makes the visualization suitable for business reporting.

💡 Why It Matters¶

Profitability is a more meaningful indicator of business success than sales alone because it reflects the actual financial value generated after business operations.

Regional profit analysis helps management identify the most profitable markets, evaluate the effectiveness of regional business strategies, and prioritize investments in areas that generate the greatest financial return.

📈 Key Observations¶

  • The Central region generated the highest total profit among all regions.
  • The North region recorded the second-highest total profit.
  • North Asia ranked third in terms of profitability.
  • Canada and Southeast Asia generated the lowest total profits.
  • Regional profitability varies considerably across different geographical markets.

🧠 Business Insights¶

The Central region not only generated the highest sales but also achieved the highest profit, indicating strong overall business performance.

Although the South region ranked second in sales, it generated lower profit than both North and North Asia, suggesting comparatively lower profit margins. Similarly, Southeast Asia produced moderate sales but very low profit, indicating potential pricing, discount, or cost-related challenges that may require further investigation.

💼 Business Recommendation¶

Management should continue strengthening operations in highly profitable regions such as Central, North, and North Asia while identifying the factors driving their strong financial performance.

For regions such as South and Southeast Asia, where profitability is relatively low compared to sales, management should evaluate pricing strategies, discount policies, product mix, and operational costs to improve profit margins.

⭐ Key Takeaway¶

The Central region is the company's most profitable market, while differences between regional sales and profit indicate opportunities to improve profitability in several regions.

✅ Analysis Summary¶

This analysis compared total profit across geographical regions and identified Central as the company's strongest profit-generating market. The findings demonstrate that regions with high sales do not always achieve proportionally high profits, emphasizing the importance of evaluating both revenue and profitability when making regional business decisions.

✅ Section Summary (5.4)¶

This section evaluated the company's regional performance by analyzing both total sales and total profit across different geographical regions.

The analysis identified the Central region as the strongest performer in terms of both revenue and profitability. It also revealed that regional sales and profits do not always increase together, highlighting the importance of considering profitability alongside revenue when evaluating market performance.

These insights provide valuable guidance for regional business planning, resource allocation, and future market expansion strategies.

➡️ Next Analysis¶

While regional analysis explains where the company performs best geographically, the next section focuses on market performance to understand how different business markets contribute to overall sales and profitability.

📊 5.5 Market Analysis¶

📖 Business Scenario¶

Organizations often operate across multiple business markets, each with distinct customer behavior, competitive conditions, and revenue potential.

Evaluating market performance helps identify the strongest business markets, assess profitability across different market segments, and support strategic decisions related to investment, expansion, and resource allocation.

This section analyzes the performance of each market using Total Sales and Total Profit to understand their contribution to the company's overall business performance.

🎯 Business Questions¶

This analysis aims to answer the following business questions:

  1. Which market generates the highest sales?

  2. Which market contributes the highest profit?

  3. Do the highest-selling markets also generate the highest profit?

  4. Which markets require greater business attention?

  5. How can market performance support future business growth strategies?

🎯 Overall Step Purpose¶

The objective of this section is to evaluate the performance of different business markets using Total Sales and Total Profit.

The analysis begins with a summary comparison of market performance, followed by separate visual analyses of sales and profitability. The findings will help identify the company's strongest business markets and support strategic decision-making for future growth.

📋 Market Performance Summary¶

The following table summarizes the performance of each business market using Total Sales and Total Profit.

This high-level comparison provides an overview of how different markets contribute to the company's overall revenue and profitability before examining sales and profit individually through visual analysis.

In [24]:
market_summary = (
    df.groupby("market")
      .agg(
          Total_Sales=("sales", "sum"),
          Total_Profit=("profit", "sum")
      )
      .round(2)
)

market_summary.style.format({
    "Total_Sales": "{:,.0f}",
    "Total_Profit": "{:,.2f}"
})
Out[24]:
  Total_Sales Total_Profit
market    
APAC 3,585,833 437,577.58
Africa 783,776 88,871.63
Canada 66,932 17,817.39
EMEA 806,184 43,897.97
EU 2,938,139 372,829.74
LATAM 2,164,687 221,643.49
US 2,297,354 286,397.02

📝 Summary Table Interpretation¶

The summary table highlights noticeable differences in performance across the company's business markets.

The APAC market generated the highest sales as well as the highest profit, making it the company's strongest-performing market. The EU market ranked second in both revenue and profitability, while Canada contributed the least to overall business performance.

These findings provide a high-level understanding of market performance and establish the foundation for the detailed sales and profit analyses presented in the following subsections.

📊 5.5.1 Sales Analysis by Market¶

🎯 Step Purpose¶

The objective of this analysis is to compare the total sales generated across different business markets.

By visualizing market-wise sales performance, we can identify the company's strongest revenue-generating markets and understand how sales are distributed across different business markets.

📊 Chart Introduction¶

The following bar chart illustrates the total sales generated by each business market.

Visualizing market-wise sales enables an easy comparison of revenue contribution across markets and helps identify the markets that play the most significant role in the company's overall sales performance.

In [25]:
plt.figure(figsize=(9,5))

plt.bar(
    market_summary.index,
    market_summary["Total_Sales"]
)

plt.title(
    "Market-wise Total Sales",
    fontsize=14,
    fontweight="bold"
)

plt.xlabel("Market", fontsize=11)

plt.ylabel("Total Sales (USD)", fontsize=11)

plt.grid(axis="y", linestyle="--", alpha=0.3)

plt.gca().yaxis.set_major_formatter(
    FuncFormatter(lambda x, pos: f"{x:,.0f}")
)

plt.tight_layout()

plt.show()

🔎 Code Explanation¶

This visualization is created using Matplotlib's bar() function to compare the total sales generated by each business market.

The chart is based on the precomputed market_summary table, where:

  • The x-axis represents the business markets.
  • The y-axis represents the total sales generated by each market.

Additional formatting, including descriptive axis labels, comma-separated number formatting, gridlines, and a clear chart title, enhances readability and makes the visualization suitable for business reporting.

💡 Why It Matters¶

Market-wise sales analysis helps organizations understand which business markets contribute most significantly to overall revenue.

Identifying high-performing and low-performing markets enables management to prioritize investments, optimize resource allocation, and develop market-specific strategies that support sustainable business growth.

📈 Key Observations¶

  • APAC generated the highest total sales among all business markets.
  • EU ranked second in terms of total sales.
  • The US recorded the third-highest sales.
  • Canada generated the lowest total sales.
  • Sales performance varies considerably across different business markets.

🧠 Business Insights¶

The APAC market represents the company's strongest revenue-generating market, indicating a substantial customer base and strong market presence.

The clear variation in market-wise sales suggests that revenue opportunities differ across markets. Lower-performing markets, particularly Canada and Africa, may require focused marketing initiatives, customer acquisition strategies, or business expansion efforts to improve sales performance.

💼 Business Recommendation¶

Management should continue strengthening its presence in high-performing markets such as APAC and EU while identifying the key success factors that contribute to their strong sales performance.

For lower-performing markets, particularly Canada and Africa, management should evaluate market potential, customer demand, and competitive conditions before implementing targeted growth strategies.

⭐ Key Takeaway¶

APAC is the company's strongest revenue-generating market, while significant differences in market performance highlight opportunities for targeted business expansion.

✅ Analysis Summary¶

This analysis compared total sales across business markets and identified APAC as the company's largest revenue contributor. The findings highlight considerable differences in market performance and provide valuable insights for market prioritization and strategic business planning.

📊 5.5.2 Profit Analysis by Market¶

🎯 Step Purpose¶

The objective of this analysis is to compare the total profit generated across different business markets.

While sales measure revenue generation, profit reflects the actual financial contribution of each market. This analysis helps identify the company's most profitable markets and supports strategic business planning.

📊 Chart Introduction¶

The following bar chart illustrates the total profit generated by each business market.

By comparing market-wise profitability, we can identify the markets that contribute the greatest financial value to the company and determine whether high sales also translate into high profits.

In [26]:
plt.figure(figsize=(9,5))

plt.bar(
    market_summary.index,
    market_summary["Total_Profit"]
)

plt.title(
    "Market-wise Total Profit",
    fontsize=14,
    fontweight="bold"
)

plt.xlabel("Market", fontsize=11)

plt.ylabel("Total Profit (USD)", fontsize=11)

plt.grid(axis="y", linestyle="--", alpha=0.3)

plt.gca().yaxis.set_major_formatter(
    FuncFormatter(lambda x, pos: f"{x:,.0f}")
)

plt.tight_layout()

plt.show()

🔎 Code Explanation¶

This visualization is created using Matplotlib's bar() function to compare the total profit generated by each business market.

The chart is based on the precomputed market_summary table, where:

  • The x-axis represents the business markets.
  • The y-axis represents the total profit generated by each market.

Additional formatting, including descriptive axis labels, comma-separated number formatting, gridlines, and a clear chart title, improves readability and makes the visualization suitable for business reporting.

💡 Why It Matters¶

Profitability is a more meaningful measure of business success than sales alone because it reflects the actual financial return generated by each market.

Market-wise profit analysis helps management identify the company's most valuable markets, evaluate the effectiveness of market strategies, and prioritize investments in markets that generate the highest financial returns.

📈 Key Observations¶

  • APAC generated the highest total profit among all business markets.
  • EU ranked second in terms of profitability.
  • The US recorded the third-highest total profit.
  • Canada generated the lowest total profit.
  • Profitability varies considerably across different business markets.

🧠 Business Insights¶

The APAC market not only generated the highest sales but also achieved the highest profit, indicating strong overall business performance and healthy profit generation.

Although the US generated higher sales than LATAM, the difference in profit is relatively smaller, suggesting that profit margins vary across markets. Similarly, Canada contributes very little to both sales and profit, indicating limited business presence in that market.

💼 Business Recommendation¶

Management should continue strengthening its presence in highly profitable markets such as APAC and EU while identifying the key factors driving their strong financial performance.

For lower-performing markets, particularly Canada and EMEA, management should evaluate market potential, pricing strategies, customer demand, and operational efficiency before implementing targeted growth initiatives.

⭐ Key Takeaway¶

APAC is the company's most profitable business market, while profitability differences across markets highlight opportunities for strategic market development.

✅ Analysis Summary¶

This analysis compared total profit across business markets and identified APAC as the company's strongest profit-generating market. The findings demonstrate that profitability differs across markets and emphasize the importance of evaluating both sales and profit when making market-level business decisions.

✅ Section Summary (5.5)¶

This section evaluated the company's market performance by analyzing both total sales and total profit across different business markets.

The analysis identified APAC as the strongest-performing market in terms of both revenue and profitability, followed by the EU market. It also highlighted noticeable differences in performance across markets, emphasizing that business opportunities and financial returns vary by market.

These findings provide valuable insights for market prioritization, strategic investment, and future business expansion decisions.

➡️ Next Analysis¶

While market analysis explains performance at a broader business level, the next section focuses on Sub-Category Analysis to identify which individual product sub-categories generate the highest sales and profits, and which ones require management attention.

📊 5.6 Sub-Category Analysis¶

📖 Business Scenario¶

While category-level analysis provides a broad overview of business performance, managers often need more detailed insights into individual product groups.

Sub-category analysis helps identify which specific product groups generate the highest sales and profits, enabling better inventory management, pricing decisions, marketing strategies, and product portfolio optimization.

This section evaluates the performance of each product sub-category using Total Sales and Total Profit.

🎯 Business Questions¶

This analysis aims to answer the following business questions:

  1. Which product sub-categories generate the highest sales?

  2. Which sub-categories contribute the highest profit?

  3. Which sub-categories perform poorly despite generating high sales?

  4. Which product groups require management attention?

  5. How can product-level insights improve business profitability?

🎯 Overall Step Purpose¶

The objective of this section is to evaluate the performance of individual product sub-categories using Total Sales and Total Profit.

The analysis begins with a summary comparison of sub-category performance, followed by separate visual analyses of sales and profitability. These insights help identify the company's strongest product groups and highlight opportunities for improving product performance.

📋 Sub-Category Performance Summary¶

The following table summarizes the performance of each product sub-category using Total Sales and Total Profit.

This comparison provides a detailed overview of how individual product groups contribute to the company's overall revenue and profitability before examining sales and profit separately through visual analysis.

In [27]:
subcategory_summary = (
    df.groupby("sub_category")
      .agg(
          Total_Sales=("sales", "sum"),
          Total_Profit=("profit", "sum")
      )
      .round(2)
      .sort_values(by="Total_Sales", ascending=False)
)

subcategory_summary.style.format({
    "Total_Sales": "{:,.0f}",
    "Total_Profit": "{:,.2f}"
})
Out[27]:
  Total_Sales Total_Profit
sub_category    
Phones 1,706,874 216,717.01
Copiers 1,509,439 258,567.55
Chairs 1,501,682 141,973.80
Bookcases 1,466,559 161,924.42
Storage 1,127,124 108,461.49
Appliances 1,011,081 141,680.59
Machines 779,071 58,867.87
Tables 757,034 -64,083.39
Accessories 749,307 129,626.31
Binders 461,952 72,449.85
Furnishings 385,609 46,967.43
Art 372,163 57,953.91
Paper 244,307 59,207.68
Supplies 243,090 22,583.26
Envelopes 170,926 29,601.12
Fasteners 83,254 11,525.42
Labels 73,433 15,010.51

📝 Summary Table Interpretation¶

The summary table reveals substantial variation in performance across the company's product sub-categories.

Phones generated the highest total sales, while Copiers produced the highest total profit, indicating exceptional profitability. Most sub-categories generated positive profits; however, Tables recorded an overall loss despite generating considerable sales revenue.
